p.title: Purpose
p.text_body: The primary purpose of this platform is to enable club coordinators to post requests for scheduling events, which would be subject to approval by the Student Council's Clubs Coordinator. [cite: 4] Post this approval, the event would be added to the platform for registration by the student body, and an email notification would be sent to all the students for the same. [cite: 5] The platform would also have dedicated pages for the clubs, which the respective club's coordinator would manage. [cite: 6]

p.title: Intended Audience and Reading Suggestions
p.text_body: The primary users of the platform are (i) students of the university, (ii) the coordinators of various clubs, (iii) the Student Council's Clubs Coordinator and (iv) a representative from the Dean of Student Affairs office (Admin). [cite: 8]

p.title: Project Scope
p.text_body: The platform will manage event scheduling and registrations for student clubs at IIITD. [cite: 1, 2, 10] It provides specialized features like club-specific pages managed by coordinators [cite: 13], administrative oversight for pending event requests [cite: 16], an incident report feature [cite: 23], and a system allowing students to request the formation of new clubs[cite: 25].

p.title: Product Perspective
p.text_body: The Student Clubs Event Management Platform is an application designed for IIITD domain users. [cite: 2, 10] All IIITD domain users should be able to log into the platform using their IIITD domain Gmail IDs. [cite: 10]

itemize.item: The platform should enable the club coordinators to schedule events for any given date and time. [cite: 11]
Students should be allowed to search for upcoming events based on their interests and view them. [cite: 20]
The platform would also have dedicated pages for the clubs, which the respective club's coordinator would manage. [cite: 13]
There should be a report feature in the platform to enable everyone to report incidents and issues. [cite: 23]
Students should be able to request for the formation of a new club. [cite: 25]

p.title: User Classes and Characteristics
p.text_body:
The following are the fundamental users of our portal: [cite: 49]
General students [cite: 50]
Club coordinators [cite: 51]
Student Council Club Coordinator [cite: 52]
Admin (Admin Personnel or DoSA) [cite: 53]

p.title: Operating Environment
p.text_body: The platform should be compatible with all major browsers like Safari, Chrome, Firefox, Brave, Microsoft Edge, etc. [cite: 39] The platform should be dynamic and should be able to adjust to varying screen resolutions and devices(like laptops, cellphones, tablets, etc.). [cite: 33]

p.title: Description and Priority
p.text_body: To allow coordinators/heads of various clubs to propose the organization of events. [cite: 160] This is handled by Club Coordinators/Heads. [cite: 159]

p.title: Stimulus/Response Sequences
p.text_body: User lands on the homepage and clicks on the "Clubs" option in the navigation bar, redirecting to the clubs' information page to locate and select their respective club from the list. [cite: 164] On the club-specific page, the user clicks on the 'Propose Event' button post which a form appears. [cite: 166] After filling out this form, the user clicks the "Submit" button. [cite: 168] The event proposal is sent for approval, and the user is redirected back to the club information page. [cite: 170, 171]

p.title: Functional Requirements
p.text_body: While applying for the same, they should be permitted to edit the event details and registration requirements. [cite: 12]

p.title: Description and Priority
p.text_body: To allow users to register for upcoming events. [cite: 114] This involves student actors. [cite: 112]

p.title: Stimulus/Response Sequences
p.text_body: User explores upcoming events from the main landing page. [cite: 117] User clicks on an event to view its details. [cite: 119] User clicks on the "Register" button. [cite: 121] If logged in, user is registered and redirected to the homepage. [cite: 123]

p.title: Functional Requirements
p.text_body: If not logged in, user is prompted to log in. [cite: 127] After the event's approval, it should be added to the platform for registration by the student body. [cite: 18]

p.title: User Interfaces
p.text_body: The platform should be developed while keeping accessibility for all users in mind and should have features like screen readers, font adjusters, etc. [cite: 32] React JS for the front-end development. [cite: 36]

p.title: Software Interfaces
p.text_body: Node JS for the back-end development with MongoDB database. [cite: 37] The platform would use Google sign-in API to allow users to log in using their IIIT Delhi domain email address. [cite: 40] Upon login, the users would be redirected to Google API's interface. [cite: 41]

p.title: Communications Interfaces
p.text_body: The platform should be able to send email notifications to all students and other users. [cite: 42]

itemize.item: The platform should withstand considerable amounts of traffic and frequent shifts in load and traffic coming its way. [cite: 29]
The platform should be responsive and have an average response time of 300 milliseconds or less under normal load. [cite: 30]
The platform should be well protected from known attacks and penetration attempts to enable the protection of user information and data, like XSS(cross-site scripting) and SQL injection. [cite: 44]
The platform should prevent packet interception and tampering with outgoing and incoming requests. [cite: 45]
The platform should verify users using 2-factor authentication and IIIT Delhi domain login should be implemented using Google's API. [cite: 46]
The user data should be stored and maintained as per the institute's policy. [cite: 47]

p.title: Design Constraints
p.text_body: The platform should be easy to maintain, and the code should be readable and well-documented. [cite: 34]

itemize.item: 1. Users: Those who will use the application. [cite: 272]
2. Students: Users who study at IIIT Delhi and are not part of the management of the university. [cite: 273]
3. General students: Users who aren't part of the management. They are the primary stakeholders of the application. [cite: 274]
4. Club coordinators: Users who are part of the management, albeit at a lower level. They are responsible for managing the club activities and hosting events. [cite: 275, 276]
5. Student council club coordinator: They are student users responsible for managing and approving the events added by club coordinators/heads. [cite: 277]
6. DoSA: Department of Student Affairs at the university. [cite: 278]
7. FMS: Facilities Management Services at IIIT Delhi. [cite: 279]
8. Admin: The user can change logins and credentials, and manage the application. [cite: 280]
9. Login: Sign into the application and let it know your details. [cite: 281]
10. Explore: Browse through the events given. [cite: 282]
11. Registering: Marking that the user 'may' be present at the event. [cite: 283]
12. Participation: Participating in an event, showing interest, and attending when it is organised. [cite: 284]
13. Credentials: information required for logging in; in this case, it is the Google account associated with the institute. [cite: 285]
14. Request: It is for requesting allowance to conduct an event. [cite: 286]
